content: We can reframe agent-orchestrated answering ensembles for AI to draw on the biological/chemical definition of an agent, designing each specialized AI answering component as a biological 'active agent' that produces targeted, context-specific effects, rather than just a discrete model waiting for orchestration prompts. The ensemble can then be orchestrated to activate only the specialized agents that match the unique causal requirements of a given query, rather than running all components in parallel.
